section: Chapter 12: How Nature Works in Harmony

questions.question: What is a habitat?
questions.correct_answer: A habitat is the natural place where an organism lives and grows.
questions.explanation: It provides food, water, shelter, and suitable conditions for survival.

questions.question: What are biotic components?
questions.explanation: Plants, animals, and microorganisms are examples of biotic components.

questions.question: What are abiotic components?
questions.explanation: Examples include air, water, soil, sunlight, and temperature.

questions.question: What is a population?
questions.correct_answer: A group of organisms of the same species living in a particular area
questions.explanation: For example, many fish of the same type living in a pond form a population.

questions.question: What is a community?
questions.correct_answer: A group of different populations living together in the same habitat
questions.explanation: Plants, animals, and microorganisms together form a community.

questions.question: What is an ecosystem?
questions.correct_answer: An ecosystem is the interaction between living organisms and their environment.
questions.explanation: It includes both biotic and abiotic components interacting together.

questions.question: What are producers?
questions.correct_answer: Organisms that make their own food
questions.explanation: Plants are producers because they prepare food through photosynthesis.

questions.question: What is a food chain?
questions.correct_answer: A sequence showing who eats whom in an ecosystem
questions.explanation: It shows the flow of energy from producers to consumers.

questions.question: What is a food web?
questions.correct_answer: A network of interconnected food chains
questions.explanation: Food webs show multiple feeding relationships in an ecosystem.

questions.question: What are decomposers?
questions.correct_answer: Organisms that break down dead plants and animals
questions.explanation: Fungi and bacteria recycle nutrients back into the soil.

notes.title: Habitat
notes.content: A habitat provides the right environmental conditions for organisms to survive and grow.

notes.title: Biotic and Abiotic Components
notes.content: Biotic components include living organisms while abiotic components include non-living environmental factors.

notes.title: Population and Community
notes.content: A population includes individuals of the same species, while a community includes different populations living together.

notes.title: Food Chain
notes.content: A food chain shows the transfer of energy from producers to consumers.

notes.title: Decomposition
notes.content: Decomposition breaks down dead organic matter and recycles nutrients into the ecosystem.

facts.fact: Every ecosystem contains both living and non-living components.
Plants are called producers because they make their own food.
Food chains are interconnected to form food webs.
Decomposers play an important role in recycling nutrients in nature.
